parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write 140-190 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: In your English class you have been talking about protecting the environment. Now your English teacher has asked you to write an essay. Write an essay using all the notes and giving reasons for your point of view.

stimulus.body:
"Every family could do far more to protect the environment at home. Do you agree?"

Notes

Write about:
1. saving energy
2. reducing rubbish
3. ............... (your own idea)

bullets: give reasons for your point of view

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write 140-190 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: You see this announcement in an English-language magazine for teenagers.

stimulus.title: GREEN AT HOME
stimulus.body: Has your family changed anything at home to help the environment? Write an article describing one change your family has made, explaining how difficult it was, and saying whether you think other families should copy it. The best articles will be published next month.

bullets: one change your family has made at home
whether other families should copy it

tasks.lead: You see this announcement on an English-language website about everyday products.

stimulus.title: Eco-product reviews wanted
stimulus.body: Have you bought something that promised to be good for the planet, such as a reusable bottle, a solar charger or refillable stationery? Write a review describing the product, saying how well it works in daily life, and explaining whether it is really better than the ordinary alternative.

bullets: how well it works in daily life
whether it is better than the ordinary alternative

tasks.lead: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend Alex.

stimulus.body:
From: Alex
Subject: Greener flat

My flatmates and I have decided to make our student flat more environmentally friendly, but we don't really know where to start. You're good at this kind of thing — what should we change first? Are there any ideas that sound good but don't actually work? And how can I persuade my laziest flatmate to join in?
Thanks,
Alex

bullets: an idea that does not really work
how to persuade the lazy flatmate
